channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Grass is extremely long and area is untidy, 12th June
item.description:
The areas of grass need cutting please and the re is an area of bushes and trees that needs trimming and tidying up
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 78, Doveney Close, St Paul's Cray, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bikes are taking up the road making it difficult to drive down Doric Way safely, 12th June
item.description:
This has been an ongoing issue for sometime and only seems to be getting worse. People park their bikes up not in the allocate space but in the road. I have gotten out the car many times to move bikes and scooters out of the way to safely drive down
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 40, Doric Way, Somers Town, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Over hanging public tree requires pruning outside 55 lynwood grove, 12th June
item.description:
Tree is growing incredibly fast and needs pruning back as branches getting in the way on walking on the pavement.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lynwood Grove, Crofton, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside 90 Brondesbury Road, NW6 the reporter says (which is opposite Lynton Road), 12th June
item.description:
A large armchair (POPs) by a tree

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brondesbury Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Medium - Up to medium size van load

item.title: Plants growing out of roadside gully, 12th June
item.description:
Blocked drain has plants growing out of it. There's also more plants growing all along the roadside pavement. There are already plenty of cracks in the roadside and roadway, they will get obviously get worse if these plants grow any bigger.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Shooters Hill Road, Blackheath
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Young tree support fixings broken and tree leaning in wind, 12th June
item.description:
The new tree outside 46/48 Portland Road has come off its support fixings in the wind and now looks like it could be bowl over. This is a shame as it has started to establish well.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Portland Road, Sundridge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Foliage issue on pole 2 Ulez camera: in line of site: RequestBorough to follow up, 12th June
item.description:
Foliage issue on pole 2 Ulez camera: in line of site: RequestBorough to follow up
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tollington Road, Holloway, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Illegal waste treatment site, 12th June
item.description:
30 Raymouth road. treatment of food waste to slurry and sending off to ad plants. collection of food waste from nearby neighbours and imperial college. I work in the company and want to keep myself anonymous. Thank you

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 32, Raymouth Road, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Construction waste obstructing Thorndale driveway sightlines, 12th June
item.description:
Open bulk bags containing construction waste, including exposed sharp metal, have been placed outside the Beechbrook House site hoarding, immediately beside the Thorndale driveway exit. As of 11 June there are now two bulk bags, one stacked on top of the other. The lower bag has been present for several days. The stacking of the bags further obstructs sightlines for vehicles exiting Thorndale onto Lubbock Road. LBB Planning officers have already acknowledged that visibility from this exit is severely restricted by the site hoarding. Please inspect urgently and require the bags to be removed from this location to avoid compromising the safety of drivers exiting Thorndale. Image 1 shows the location beside the Thorndale driveway. Image 2 shows the open bulk bag contents. Image 3 shows the effect on driver visibility when exiting Thorndale.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lubbock Road, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Missing street nameplate for Erin Close, BR1 4NX, 12th June
item.description:
Street nameplate for Erin Close, Bromley BR1 4NX is missing at the junction with Elstree Hill. USRN: 20302243 – Maintainable at public expense – London Borough of Bromley according to findmystreet.co.uk Please arrange a replacement to the standard Bromley format. The missing sign makes the close hard to find for emergency services and deliveries. In case findmystreet.co.uk is incorrect and Erin Close is not maintainable by public expense please could I request a confirmation of this? Thank you

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Elstree Hill, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Foliage issues: ESP not accessible, pole 1 phases A and B :Request Borough to follow up and trim, 12th June
item.description:
Foliage issues: ESP not accessible, pole 1 phases A and B :Request Borough to follow up and trim
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 119, Hornsey Road, Holloway, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned vehicle, 12th June
item.description:
This van has been here since April 2026 with a wheel lock, it has numerous parking tickets on it and has been abandoned, the number plates have been ripped off and placed on the windscreen.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): King Edwards Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti, 11th June
item.description:
Wall and bins Thank you!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): London Road, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bike post cut open to steal bikes by The Marquess Tavern, 11th June
item.description:
Standing with The Marquess Tavern on your right, at the top of Canonbury Street, where the bike posts are by New River Walk, at least 1 bike post has been subtly cut open, to steal bikes whilst locked to the bike post.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): New River Walk, Islington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Very dirty and overgrown path, 11th June
item.description:
The path leading to the canal (the wheelchair accessible side) is so dirty with poos, litter and loads of overgrown and unkept vegetation. It is striking how this side of the canal is being neglected. I use this path every day as it is on the right side of the pavement to go to my office and it is very unkept and neglected.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Regent's Canal towpath, Barnsbury, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumping yard detected, 11th June
item.description:
Please do something about this dumping yard.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Maple Close, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The overgrown trees are obstructing the pavement, next to 73 Bow Lane, 11th June
item.description:
The overgrown trees are obstructing the pavement, making it impassable in places. As a result, pedestrians, including parents with buggies, elderly people, wheelchair users, and others with mobility difficulties, are forced to step into the road in order to pass safely.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Granville Road, Fallow Corner, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lampost is bent, 11th June
item.description:
Lampost was hit and now stands at at angle, has been like it for a month or so

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Havelock Road, Upper Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Loading sign at angle, 11th June
item.description:
The loading sign outside cavendish apartments 16A wyndham road apreents 2-42 is at an angle. Perhaps a lorry hit it and knocked it, please send someone to fix it? thanks

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Wyndham Road, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken slide, 11th June
item.description:
This broken slide has been reported in April when is it going to be repaired? The local children have very little to play with in this park and have now waited many weeks for this repair. On behalf of the children Can we get it fixed soon please 🙏

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): St. Timothy's Mews, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
